title: Manufacturing of Living Building Materials With Calcifying Cyanobacteria
author: Patrick Jung
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML
materials: Workflow for manufacturing living building materials (LBMs) with cyanobacteria. Schematic overview including the most relevant steps such as cultivation of cyanobacteria, preparation of ALS-gel, casting and demolding of prisms, and the evaluation of bending tensile and compressive strength.
A+ trace components is heat sensitive.
Magnetic stirring bar (VWR Collection, catalog number: 442-
Sterile indicator strip (Roth, catalog number: XC20.

steps.title: Picosynechococcus sp
steps.instruction: Picosynechococcus sp. PCC 7002 from Pasteur Culture Collection [11] Reagents

steps.title: A+ trace components [12] Component Reagent...
steps.instruction: A+ trace components [12] Component Reagent Stock solution (g/100 mL) Nutrient solution 1 H 3 BO 3 3.43 g/L 2 ZnCl 2 0.0315 g/L 3 MoO 3 0.003 g/L 4 Vitamin B12 0.0004 g/L 5 FeCl 3 ·6H 2 O 3.89 1 mL/L 6 MnCl 2 ·4H 2 O 4.3 1 mL/L 7 CuSO 4 ·5H 2 O 0.003 1 mL/L 8 CoCl 2 ·6H 2 O 0.012 1 mL/L a. To prepare 1 L of A+ trace components, add 500 mL of dH 2 O to a 1 L glass bottle and add 3.43 g of component 1, 0.0315 g of component 2, 0.003 g of component 3, and 0.0004 g of component 4 as described above while stirring continuously. b. Prepare 100 mL of each stock solution from components 5–

steps.title: Use Milli-Q or dH 2 O for each solution
steps.instruction: Use Milli-Q or dH 2 O for each solution. c. Then, add 1 mL of each stock solution from components 5–8 above and adjust to 1 L by adding dH 2 O. d. Store all stock solutions and A+ trace components in a refrigerator.

steps.title: A+ medium [12] Component Reagent Stock...
steps.instruction: A+ medium [12] Component Reagent Stock solution (g/100 mL) Nutrient solution 1 NaCl 18 g/L 2 MgSO 4 ·7H 2 O 5 g/L 3 Na 2 EDTA·2H 2 O 0.3 10 mL/L 4 KCl 6 10 mL/L 5 CaCl 2 ·2H 2 O 3.7 10 mL/L 6 NaNO 3 10 10 mL/L 7 KH 2 PO 4 0.5 10 mL/L 8 Trizma base pH 8.2 10 10 mL/L 9 A+ trace components 10 mL/L a. Prepare 100 mL of each stock solution from components 3–8 from the table above. Use Milli-Q or dH 2 O for each solution. b. To prepare 1 L of A+ medium, add 500 mL of dH 2 O in a 1 L glass bottle and add 18 g of component 1, 5 g of component 2, and 10 mL of each stock solution of components 3–8 as shown above, while stirring continuously. Note: Store all stock solutions in the dark at room temperature. c. Fill up to 1 L with dH 2 O and adjust pH to 7.6 with HCl solution. d. Sterilize by autoclaving (121 °C, 15 psi, 20 min). e. Add 10 mL of A+ trace components (see Recipe

steps.title: For agar plates, A+ medium is...
steps.instruction: For agar plates, A+ medium is supplemented with sodium thiosulfate pentahydrate (24.819 g/mL) stock solution; 1 mL/L A+ medium and 1.5% (w/v) agarose. f. Store the final A+ medium at room temperature before usage.

steps.title: A+ modified medium [6] Component Reagent...
steps.instruction: A+ modified medium [6] Component Reagent Stock solution (g/100 mL) Nutrient solution 1 MgSO 4 ·7H 2 O 5 g/L 2 Na 2 EDTA·2H 2 O 0.3 10 mL/L 3 KCl 6 10 mL/L 4 CaCl 2 ·2H 2 O 3.7 10 mL/L 5 NaNO 3 10 10 mL/L 6 KH 2 PO 4 0.5 10 mL/L 7 Trizma base pH 8.2 10 10 mL/L 8 A+ trace components 10 mL/L Note: NaCl is left out to avoid halite formation. Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Pipette tips with filter (Mettler Toledo, model
steps.instruction: Pipette tips with filter (Mettler Toledo, model: RAININ)

steps.title: Check valves (Brand, catalog number
steps.instruction: Check valves (Brand, catalog number: 229-

steps.title: Set the 1 L culture flasks...
steps.instruction: Set the 1 L culture flasks together by cutting silicon pipes into the desired length and mounting them on the olives of the lids. Install the check valves at the intake part and a sterile filter at the outlet olive and close the laboratory glass bottles. Cover the open ends of the silicon pipes with aluminum foil.

steps.title: Sterilize the prepared culture flask by...
steps.instruction: Sterilize the prepared culture flask by autoclaving (121 °C, 15 psi, 20 min) and let it cool to room temperature.

steps.title: Open the culture flask under sterile...
steps.instruction: Open the culture flask under sterile conditions and inoculate Picosynechococcus sp. PCC 7002 in 500 mL of A+ medium. Close the culture flask after inoculation.

steps.title: Grow Picosynechococcus sp
steps.instruction: Grow Picosynechococcus sp. PCC 7002 under discontinuous light illumination (~30 μmol m -2 ·s -1 ; full spectrum; 18/6 h using a time switch) and slight pressurized air supply (using the infrastructure of the institution, e.g., by connecting tubes to an air compressor with oil filter) for mixing at 25 °C until the stationary phase (OD 750 >

steps.title: Collect 400 mL of liquid bacterial...
steps.instruction: Collect 400 mL of liquid bacterial culture equilibrated to OD 750 = 0.3 (sufficient for 2 molds, each containing 3 prisms) under sterile conditions. Centrifuge this at 7,000× g for 2 min at room temperature. Discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Fill 400 mL of A+ modified...
steps.instruction: Fill 400 mL of A+ modified medium in a 1 L laboratory glass bottle and heat it up to 45 °C on a magnetic stirrer under continuous mixing at 300 rpm.

steps.title: Dissolve 40 g of gelatin in...
steps.instruction: Dissolve 40 g of gelatin in the solution mentioned above until a transparent, yellowish, homogenous solution is obtained.

steps.title: Cool the solution down to 40...
steps.instruction: Cool the solution down to 40 °C and slowly add 3.36 g of NaHCO 3 .

steps.title: Adjust pH to 7
steps.instruction: Adjust pH to 7.6 (use HCl to reduce pH and NaOH to raise pH).

steps.title: Add 5.73 g of CaCl 2 ·2H 2 O. Caution
steps.instruction: Add 5.73 g of CaCl 2 ·2H 2 O. Caution : Reduce the stirring speed to avoid the formation of foam.

steps.title: Cool the solution down to 37...
steps.instruction: Cool the solution down to 37 °C and add the cell pellet.

steps.title: Incubate the solution under continuous, artificial...
steps.instruction: Incubate the solution under continuous, artificial full-spectrum light (180 μmol m -2 ·s -1 ) and moderate stirring for 10 h at 37 °C. Note: Set the temperature slightly below 37 °C to avoid overshoot. C. Casting and demolding of the prisms

steps.title: Apply a thin layer of organic...
steps.instruction: Apply a thin layer of organic virgin rapeseed oil to the steel three-gang mold using a brush.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.35 kg of ISO standard sand (one package for 1 × 3 prisms) to a metal bowl and gently mix it with 198.50 g of prepared ALS-gel (for 1 × 3 prisms) using a spatula. Caution : To keep a constant grain size distribution regarding the ISO standard sand, prepare the mixture for each 1 × 3 prism mold individually. Note: There is a leftover of approximately 50 mL of incubated ALS-gel when preparing 2 × 3 prisms.

steps.title: Discontinuously mix the ALS-gel-sand by hand...
steps.instruction: Discontinuously mix the ALS-gel-sand by hand at room temperature until it starts to become slightly viscous (e.g., once every 5 min after initial thorough mixing). Caution : Do not mix the ALS-gel-sand too often since air bubbles and the formation of foam negatively influence the stability of the prisms.

steps.title: Fill the mixture into the steel...
steps.instruction: Fill the mixture into the steel mold by pressing it with the spatula.

steps.title: Leave the mold in the fridge...
steps.instruction: Leave the mold in the fridge for at least 6 h at 8 °C. Note: As the mixture cools down, it physically cross-links and is able to maintain structural integrity, which is sufficient for demolding.

steps.title: Detach the molds and allow the...
steps.instruction: Detach the molds and allow the prisms to dry on metal grates at ambient temperature (20–22 °C; 40%–60% relative humidity; protected from direct sunlight) for at least 14 days and until mass equilibrium has been reached. Figure

description: Validation of protocol For the validation of this protocol ( Figure 1 ), six prisms were prepared as described using Picosynechococcus sp. PCC 7002, as well as six prisms without cyanobacteria as a control. After no weight loss was detected for the prisms (after 14 days), they were tested for bending tensile and compressive strength. Therefore, each block was mounted on a universal testing machine with an integrated load cell (ZwickRoell) according to DIN EN 196-1:2016-11 (German version of a European standard; this standard sets out the procedure for preparing, curing, and testing mortar specimens made with cement, in order to determine compressive—and optionally tensile—strength). Mechanical properties were tested via three-point bending tensile test (50 ± 10 N/s; load-controlled), followed by uniaxial compressive failure test (2,400 ± 200 N/s) with three replicates each. As a result, a significant difference between the prisms with and without cyanobacteria was detected (Figure 2) for bending tensile strength (t = 2.9717; p = 0.0180) and a highly significant difference for compressive strength (t = 5.75; p = 0.00002). The results show the effect of cyanobacterial calcification on the mechanical properties of the living building materials. Figure 2. Bending tensile and compressive strengths of three prisms. Independent two-sample Welch’s t-test (n = 6, each). * indicates statistical significance.
